sourceMovie.title: The Purge: Anarchy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Purge: Anarchy (2014) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. James DeMonaco's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Purge: Election Year
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Purge: Anarchy clicked for you, The Purge: Election Year (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Purge: Anarchy?
faq.answer: Try The Purge: Election Year, The Forever Purge, The Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Beginning—each shares DNA with The Purge: Anarchy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Purge: Anarchy?
faq.answer: The Purge: Anarchy (2014) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Purge: Anarchy part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Purge Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
